incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect numbers on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. A turn-around fact swaps the order of the factors.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect numbers on second attempt

params.description: The factors 9 and 4 highlight in the given equation.

steps.text: Here's a hint: the factors in the turn-around fact are the same, just switched. Look at the two factors in 9 × 4.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect numbers three or more times. System models the answer.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The given fact is 9 times 4 equals 36. The turn-around fact swaps the order: 4 times 9 equals 36. The answer is 4 × 9 = 36.
